pages.text:
Overview
Identity governance minimizes standing privileged access by combining Microsoft Entra
ID's directory and Conditional Access engine with Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for
time-bound role elevation — the foundation of a defensible just-in-time (JIT) access model
for both cloud admin and application-level privileges.
Entra ID Foundations
Concept
Details
Tenant
Dedicated, isolated directory instance — top-level identity boundary
Conditional Access
Policy engine evaluating signals (user, device, location, risk) to grant/
block/require MFA
P1 vs P2 licensing
P1: Conditional Access, group-based access; P2 adds PIM, Identity
Protection, access reviews
Break-glass
accounts
Cloud-only, excluded from Conditional Access, monitored, credentials
sealed/offline
Identity Protection
Risk-based sign-in/user risk scoring feeding Conditional Access risk
policies
Access Reviews
Periodic, owner-driven recertification of role/group membership
PIM — Just-In-Time Privileged Access
Eligible vs. Active assignment — eligible roles require activation; active roles are
standing (avoid for admin roles)
Activation controls — require MFA, justification, ticket number, and/or approver sign-
off before elevation
Time-bound activation — role auto-expires after a defined window (e.g., 1–8 hours),
forcing re-activation for continued use
C L O U D C H E AT S H E E T · I D E N T I T Y
Identity Governance & JIT Access
Entra ID, Privileged Identity Management, and just-in-time privileged access architecture
• 
• 
• 
Mazhar Khan — Cloud Security Portfolio
Page 1 of 3

PIM for Groups — extends JIT activation to group membership, cascading to any
resource the group is assigned to
Alerts — PIM flags roles activated too frequently, assigned outside PIM, or with stale
permissions
Access Reviews for PIM roles — periodic recertification of who holds eligible
assignments
JIT Architecture Pattern (Cloud-Agnostic)
Request (user submits justification, often via ticket/portal) → Approval workflow (automated for low-
risk, human approver for high-risk roles) → Time-bound grant (PIM activation or a short-lived IAM role/
credential, e.g. via a Lambda/Function-based approval automation) → Automatic revocation at expiry
→ Full audit trail of request, approval, and usage for compliance evidence.
AWS/Cross-Cloud JIT Equivalents
AWS IAM Identity Center + permission sets — time-limited session duration on
assumed roles
STS AssumeRole with short session tokens — foundation for custom JIT approval
automation (e.g., Lambda-triggered)
Just-in-time EC2 access — Systems Manager Session Manager removes standing SSH/
RDP exposure entirely
GCP — Privileged Access Manager (PAM) provides analogous time-bound, approval-
gated role grants
Governance Best Practices
Default to zero standing access for privileged roles — everything eligible, nothing
permanently active
Separate break-glass accounts from PIM entirely — they must work even if PIM/
Conditional Access is misconfigured
Enforce MFA + Conditional Access as a prerequisite for any PIM activation, not just
initial sign-in
Route all activations through centralized logging (Entra sign-in/audit logs → Sentinel)
for anomaly detection
Pair PIM with access reviews on a recurring cadence to catch role sprawl and unused
eligibility
• 
• 
• 
• 
• 
• 
•

• 
• 
•

Mazhar Khan — Cloud Security Portfolio
Page 2 of 3

Common Pitfalls
Pitfall
Fix
Privileged roles
assigned as Active
by default
Default every privileged role to Eligible; require PIM activation for use
No approval
required for
sensitive role
activation
Require approver and justification for Global Admin-tier and similar
high-impact roles
Break-glass
accounts subject to
Conditional Access
Explicitly exclude break-glass accounts from CA policies; monitor for
any sign-in
PIM activation with
no time limit tuning
Set the shortest practical activation window per role sensitivity
Access reviews
configured but never
actioned
Assign accountable reviewers and track completion; auto-remove on
non-response
Interview / Exam Quick Hits
PIM requires Entra ID P2 licensing — P1 alone does not include just-in-time role
activation
Eligible assignments show zero standing privilege in access reviews until explicitly
activated
Break-glass accounts should be excluded from Conditional Access to guarantee
emergency access if policy misfires
PIM activation approval can be multi-stage — different approvers for different
sensitivity tiers of role
Combine PIM with Conditional Access authentication context to require step-up
auth only at the moment of privileged action
